feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Process Integration
feed.subtitle: SAP Process Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How can an inbound REST GET/POST request from a backend system be conditionally routed in SAP PI/PO
entry.content:
How can an inbound REST GET/POST request from a backend system be conditionally routed in SAP PI/PO based on the presence of an HTTP header parameter (ucasurl), such that:

  • If the HTTP header parameter ucasurl is present, the message is routed to a receiver REST channel that:

    • Uses a dynamic target URL derived from the value of ucasurl, and

    • Executes an HTTP GET call; and

  • If the HTTP header parameter ucasurl is not present, the message is routed to an alternative receiver REST channel that:

    • Uses a static, preconfigured target URL, and

    • Executes an HTTP POST call?

entry.title: Elimination of SAP PI PO systems
entry.content:
Hello. I'm creating this post so we can all share our opinions constructively.

I recently learned that SAP plans to eliminate SAP PI/PO systems to focus solely on Integration Suite.

As someone who works extensively with PO, I feel saddened and angry with SAP. After all these years of
working and continuously acquiring knowledge in PO, SAP seems to have decided to kick it to the curb.
All that knowledge feels like throwing it away. Similarly, SAP is forcing clients who have already invested
money in implementing PO to migrate to Integration Suite. I believe many clients don't want the cloud and prefer
to keep their servers on-premises for security or other reasons. However, SAP doesn't care and is forcing all
clients to move their infrastructure to the cloud... and of course, spend money again.
Personally, I find this despicable.

And although I work with PO, I also work with Integration Suite. I find IS awful, much more difficult,
since you have to develop the business logic by creating huge and complicated iFlows, while in PO,
integrations are generally point-to-point, making them easier to implement and understand.
And then there are options like the Monitor, which is rubbish compared to the PO monitor, where it shows
you all the messages, their log traces, payloads... without you having to do anything, by default.
In IS, if you want to see anything in the monitor, you have to develop it yourself to save headers,
log files, payloads, etc., wasting your time.

Anyway, I find it incredible that SAP has played this game with its customers, forcing them to switch.
And of course, it's forcing us, the employees, to learn another way of doing integrations, throwing away
our knowledge. (I just hope SAP doesn't do very well.)

What do you think?
What's your opinion?
Are you for or against it?

Let's see if we can start a thread to discuss this and get it to someone at SAP.

Thanks. Regards.

entry.title: Transfer objects created in Integration Suite to Cloud Integration NEO. (Message-type and data-type)
entry.content:
Hi everyone !

    I'm working on a project migrating SAP PI/PO to Cloud Integration NEO (the migration from NEO to Cloud Foundry will be next year). To perform the migration using the migration tool, following the instructions provided by SAP, we created a BTP Trial account and imported all the objects from PI/PO. After the migration, I'm transferring the objects and iFlows to BTP NEO (Cloud Integration). However, Cloud Integration NEO doesn't have the option to import Data-Types or Message-Types that were created during the migration in the Integration Suite of the Trial account. My question is: has anyone done this transfer from Integration Suite to Cloud Integration NEO before? How do I transfer the data-types and message-types to NEO? And if I have to create them manually in NEO, how do I create these objects in their iFlows on Cloud Integration NEO ?

entry.title: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty - SAP S4 to Biztalk 2016
entry.content:
Hello Team,

While Connecting from SAP S4 Cloud to MS Biztalk 2016 it is failing with below error. Totally stucked up with this error for few weeks. SAP states to uplift SAP NCO connector to 3.1.6 it doesn't really help.

 

Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty - Stucked in SM58

Latest Update from SAP- Really issue is strange and fishy!!!

Logs:

 

MS REPLY:

 

 

 

 

Thanks and Regards,

Rajesh PS

entry.title: Exploring Middleware Options for SAP Public Cloud to OFTP2 Integration
entry.content:
HI SAP Friends.

Exploring Middleware Options for SAP Public Cloud to OFTP2 Integration

In one of our current engagements with a Europe-based client, we are managing a business scenario involving approximately 35 interfaces. These interfaces are responsible for integrating the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system with an OFTP2 adapter, currently facilitated through SEEBURGER.

As part of our evaluation, we are exploring alternative solutions—either within the SAP ecosystem or through third-party applications—that can provide similar OFTP2 communication capabilities as SEEBURGER. The objective is to identify a scalable, secure, and cost-effective middleware solution that can efficiently handle multiple interfaces while ensuring compliance with OFTP2 standards.

We are particularly interested in solutions that offer:

  • Seamless integration with SAP Public Cloud
  • Native or supported OFTP2 capabilities
  • Strong monitoring and error-handling features
  • Flexibility to scale across multiple interfaces

If you have experience with similar integration scenarios or can recommend suitable alternatives, your insights would be highly valuable.

entry.title: Is it possible to connect two XI adapters to the same SAP backend (PI and Integration Suite)?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

we are currently evaluating a migration from SAP PI/PO to the SAP Integration Suite.

In our current setup, several interfaces use ABAP proxies via the XI adapter.

During the transition phase we are wondering:

  • Is it technically possible to connect two XI adapter endpoints to the same SAP backend system as sender and receiver?
    • one pointing to PI/PO
    • one pointing to Integration Suite

Questions:

  1. Can an SAP backend system maintain two XI adapter connections simultaneously?
  2. How would the proxy decide which endpoint to use (logical port, configuration, etc.)?
  3. Are there any best practices or limitations for this setup during migration?

Any experiences or recommendations would be highly appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: How to automate the daily monitoring report for PO interface
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are looking to automate the SAP PO daily monitoring report to avoid manual creation. Please let me know how can we achieve  this or what are the things from SAP PO side  needed. Please explain in detail. Kindly find the attached report format.

entry.title: SPROXY shows “Service outdated” only for newly created Integration Suite Service Interfaces
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

we are currently testing ABAP proxy generation with SAP Integration Suite.

We observed the following behavior in transaction SPROXY:

  • Migrated PI/PO service interfaces can be activated and do not show this issue
  • New service interfaces created directly in SAP Integration Suite can also be used to generate ABAP proxies
  • Runtime communication works: the ABAP proxy can receive messages from Integration Suite
  • However, in SPROXY these newly created interfaces remain in status “Service outdated”
  • Regeneration and activation of the proxy do not remove the status

 

So the issue seems to occur only for service interfaces modeled natively in Integration Suite, not for interfaces migrated from PI/PO.

Questions:

  1. Is this a known limitation or bug of ABAP proxy generation with newly created Integration Suite service interfaces?
  2. Can the “Service outdated” status be ignored if runtime communication is already working?
  3. Has anyone managed to get rid of this status for newly created interfaces?

Any experience would be greatly appreciated.

KR

Stefan

entry.title: ALRTCATDEF - How to change the error message format from action FM (MDG)
entry.content:
Hi,

Kindly help to get the ALRTCATDEF message in proper format.
I have done the ALRTCATDEF setup in my MDG system. In the action FM I am filling my return_tab with error messages.
If return_tab has error messges then AIF alert is triggered and also mail is triggered. The mail is configured in ALRTCATDEF in long/short message text tab. There, I am using container object to fill the message. Container object has message with type bapiret2. I am using that and only specified message filed with multiple lines as continuous text (&messages[].message[]&).

But when I get mail, whole bapiret2 structure also comes in mail.
Example: <msg_type> <msg_class> <msg_num> <message> <msg_v1> <msg_v2> etc.

(E ZMSG_CLS 009 Error in CR Request Error in CR Request)

But expectation is just the <message> since I added only the message field (&messages[].message[]&) like: Error in CR Request.

Did someone encountered such issue or this is just standard and SAP processes all the fields of return_tab?

If not please help me configure it correctly. Please fell free to ask any inputs.

Thanks,
Rahul

entry.title: Using REST polling for pulling file from Azure blob storage PI/PO 7.5 SP 34
entry.content:
Hi,

Is it possible to pull a file from Azure blob storage using REST sender with REST Polling. I have tried with SAS URL, but getting error "HTTP GET call to htpps://XXX not successful. Server failed to authenticate the request. Make sure the value of Authorization header is formed correctly including the signature"

The authorization values in SAS URL is as follows "sv=2025-11-05&ss=bfqt&srt=o&sp=rwdlacupiytfx&se=2027-04-21"

I am using x-ms-version 2022-11-02 in HTTP Header

The call with the same URL from Postman is successful.

Is the REST Sender adapter capable of pulling file from Azure storage?

Is there any additional configuration other than the above required for making this work.

Thank you

entry.title: EXCEPTION SEGMENT UNKNOWN RAISED error while calling RFC IDOCTYPE_READ_COMPLETE in Biztalk Middlewar
entry.content:
Hello Mates,

Good Day!

Currently we are facing issue when importing the extended idocs from SAP S4 to biztalk with IDOC segment release 701. We tried changing the WE20 outbound partner profiles parameter idoc release to 701 and also in T-code WE82 but no luck. All segments are in released stated and unlocked and also verified AUTH Issue.

I highly doubt the feasibility and compatability if SAP S4 actually accepts 701 release or not?!!!

Flow:

SAP S4 (idoc extension with 701 release)  -> MS Biztalk -> EDI Partners

Error:

Error while retrieving or generating the WSDL. Adapter message: Details: ErrorCode=RFC_EXCEPTION. ErrorGroup=RFC_ERROR_APPLICATION_EXCEPTION. SapErrorMessage=EXCEPTION SEGMENT_UNKNOWN RAISED.  AdapterErrorMessage=Error returned by RfcCallReceiveEx while calling RFC: IDOCTYPE_READ_COMPLETE..

 

Screenshot:

Thanks and Regards,

Rajesh PS

entry.title: Looking for SAP PO ESR & ID TPZ/ZIP metadata exports
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I currently do not have access to an SAP PO system, but I am looking for sample SAP PO metadata exports from a non-productive or UAT environment for testing and learning purposes.

I am interested in both:

  • ESR metadata exports

  • Integration Directory (ID) metadata exports

Especially TPZ/ZIP export examples, interface configurations, mappings, communication channels, and integration scenarios.

If anyone can help, share examples, or suggest public resources, I would highly appreciate it.

Thank you in advance for your support.

SAP Cloud Integration for data services  SAP Process Integration

entry.title: Exact action for ESR Message mapping transformation test
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Following is our SAP PO/PI version and Can someone guide me the exact action to be assigned to the custom role to an user? We wanted to restrict the user in such a way he should not be able to edit the existing objects in ESR...Thank you!

 

Make-ReleaseNW750EXT_32_REL
SPS-Nummer32
JDK-Versionjdk18

Thanks, Ramu.

entry.title: Where can I see SXI_MONITOR errors after successful retry?
entry.content:
In our ECC system we have orders going out via PI to a separate TM system. Normally they process immediately but for the example below in SXI_MONITOR there was a 3 minute delay:

And in the details I can see a retry count of 1 which likely means there was an initial error and then it automatically retried successfully:

Normally if I see an error that hasn't reprocessed I can see error information but for this one I can't find anything. Is there somewhere I can find this information?

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM | Implementation | CDM | SAP Launchpad Application
entry.content:
In a SAP Cloud ALM tenant, one of the landscapes has been set up with the change and deployment management module to manage transport requirements for SAP S4HANA, SAP CPI systems, and the SAP launchpad system. 

 

For above mentioned CPI and Launchpad system, the SAP cloud transport management system (CTMS) is already integrated into the SAP cloud alm tool via the SAP BTP cockpit.

CTS is enabled for S4HANA system.

 

I can view all the systems of above-mentioned sap applications in the landscape management of SAP cloud ALM administration Console. 

 

The system groups and deployment plans are in place. In system group for launchpad system we have tried with two products as SAP BTP cloud foundry environment  and SAP build work zone, standard edition

For the  SAP CPI we have selected product as SAP integration suite.

 

We can create features and add transport for the S/4HANA and CPI systems in the Features app.

However, when adding transport for the Launchpad system and selecting the 'Assign' button in the Features app, none of the transports of  Launchpad systems are getting listed. Rest of the systems S4HANA and CPI are listed, but the Launchpad system is not.  

 

Checked following :

Flag of “controlled by Solution Manager” is not set for Dev and UAT system, though for the prod system is set, but as of now not able to view the Dev or UAT in the list while assigning the transport.

Same CTMS is already leveraged for CPI systems, and no issues are observed nor encounter any errors for CPI transports.

In LMS of Cloud ALM, the attempt to create endpoint creation is throwing error stating endpoint creation is not required for SAP build work zone standard edition as none of the CALM use cases requires endpoint.

entry.title: How to remove json‘s outer braces with setIgnoredElements in rest adapter receive channel?
entry.content:
Hi guys,  

we use rest adapter for receive channel to post json format message to target system for some times. If need to remove some  element from the message before post to target system, just add the module parameter setIgnoredElements. It works well.

But, recently, we get into a trouble with it, when post as message like below example:    

<ns:MT_dataFromSAP xmlns:ns0="http://example.com/ERP">
     <customCode>12</customCode>
     <item>
               <field1>10</field1>
              <field2>123843892842</field2>
    </item>
    <item>
             <field1>20</field1>
             <field2>22222</field2>
    </item>
</ns:MT_dataFromSAP>

  after written the value of customCode to message header, we need to remove it from the payload, and we expect to post the converted json message like below shown:

            [   {       "field1": "10",       "field2": "123843892842"   },   {       "field1": "20",       "field2": "22222"    } ]

So，set the parameter setIgnoredElements with value "customCode,item".

unexpectly, when do test, the converted output message is:

       {[     {   "field1": "10",  "field2": "123843892842" },    {  "field1": "20",  "field2": "22222"    } ]}

So， how can we remove the outer braces "{}" ?

Appreciate it if u can give some advice.

entry.title: AS2 Partner ID Reuse Across Multiple Partner Profiles in SAP CPI TPM
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I’m fairly new to SAP CPI Trading Partner Management (TPM) and I’ve run into a situation I don’t know how to solve.

  • I have 10 partners who all use the same AS2 Partner ID (SIBSERVICE1005), the same AS2 connection, and the same certificate.

  • In TPM, the AS2 Partner ID must be unique per Partner Profile, so CPI doesn’t allow me to assign SIBSERVICE1005 to more than one profile.

  • If I change the AS2 ID to keep them unique, the actual process becomes incorrect, because all partners technically use the same AS2 ID and certificate in reality.

Since I’m still exploring TPM, I’d really appreciate guidance from the community:

  1. How can I configure TPM so that multiple partners can share the same AS2 ID and certificate?

  2. Is the recommended approach to create one Partner Profile with the shared AS2 ID and then manage partner‑specific logic at the Agreement level?

  3. Are there any best practices for handling certificate reuse across multiple agreements or partners in this scenario?

Any guidance or examples from your experience would be very helpful.

Thanks in advance,

entry.title: SAP HCM to Third Party Integration (LIve)
entry.content: SAP HCM master data needs to be updated in our third-party systems.  How can this be achieved in real time? We are not able to find any time stamp or event triggered at the time of data change/update in infotypes? What is the SAP recommended approach for real-time HR master data update propagation to external systems? We are unable to find an SAP note.

entry.title: SAP PO 7.5 REST Receiver - Capture HTTP 500 response in Asynchronous Interface and send via alert
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have an asynchronous interface in SAP PO 7.5 from SAP to D365(proxy to rest) using the REST Receiver Adapter.

When the target REST API (D365) returns HTTP 500, the response contains a useful JSON error like this:

{
"Message": "Receipt processing failed for Purchase Order "5*********"
}
I can see this response in the Message Monitor under the server response, but it is not available as the XI message payload.

My requirement is to automatically send this business error to users by email instead of manually copying it from Message Monitor.

My questions are:

Can SAP PO capture the HTTP 500 response body in a standard asynchronous REST receiver?
Can the REST Result Handler be used to extract the Message field and use it for email notifications?
If not, what is the SAP-recommended approach?

entry.title: Is it possible to dynamically determine OAuth Client ID and Client Secret in SAP PI/PO REST Adapter?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a requirement in SAP Process Integration / Process Orchestration (SAP PI/PO 7.5) and would appreciate your guidance on the recommended approach.

We are using the REST Receiver Adapter with OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials Grant to call an external REST API.

The communication channel is configured with OAuth authentication, where the following parameters are maintained statically in the receiver communication channel:

  • OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials Grant
  • Authorization Server URL
  • Scope
  • Client ID
  • Client Secret

The current design works correctly. However, we have a scalability requirement.

Current Requirement

We need to integrate approximately 30 different company codes.

Each company code has:

  • Its own Client ID
  • Its own Client Secret
  • The same OAuth flow and API structure

At the moment, because the OAuth credentials are maintained in the communication channel, we would need to create separate PI/PO configurations for every company code.

This would result in maintaining approximately:

  • 3 ESR objects per company code
  • 3 Communication Channels per company code

For around 30 company codes, this means maintaining approximately 90 ESR objects and 90 Communication Channels, which significantly increases implementation effort and ongoing maintenance.

What we are looking for

We would like to know whether there is a standard SAP-supported approach to dynamically determine the OAuth Client ID and Client Secret at runtime based on the incoming company code (or another business parameter), instead of maintaining separate communication channels.

Some possible approaches we were considered are:

  • Dynamic Configuration
  • Adapter Modules
  • Java Mapping
  • User-Defined Functions
  • Lookup from a database or secure credential store
  • Any other SAP-recommended mechanism

Questions

  1. Is it possible to make the Client ID and Client Secret dynamic in the PI/PO REST Receiver Adapter?
  2. Can the REST Adapter retrieve OAuth credentials dynamically during runtime?
  3. Has anyone implemented a similar scenario where multiple business entities use different OAuth credentials while minimizing the number of communication channels?
  4. What would be the SAP-recommended best practice for this type of requirement?

Our goal is to keep the solution scalable and maintainable while following SAP best practices and avoiding a large number of ESR objects and Communication Channels.

Any implementation experience, SAP Notes, blogs, or documentation references would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

Praveen de Silva
